[[Will Pryce]] (nephew) }} }} '''Karl Pryce''' (born 27 July 1986), also known by the [[nickname]]s of '''"Big Karl"''', and '''"[[List of Police Academy characters#Cadet/Off./Sgt./Lt./Capt. Moses Hightower|Hightower]]"''', is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer, best known for playing for the [[Bradford Bulls]]. His usual position was on the {{rlp|wg}}. Pryce previously played [[rugby league]] with [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] and [[London Broncos|Harlequins RL]]. He also played [[rugby union]] with [[Gloucester RFC|Gloucester]]. ==Background== Pryce was born in [[Bradford]], [[West Yorkshire]], England, he is the younger brother of former [[England national rugby league team|England]] and [[Catalans Dragons]] [[rugby league]] player, [[Leon Pryce]]. He is of [[Jamaicans|Jamaican]] descent.{{Cite web|url=http://www.sportsjamaica.com/read_article.php?id=3696|title = Sports Jamaica:: World Champions back Rugby League in the WI:: Rugby}} ==Playing career== ===2000s=== He made his début at St Helens in 2003 and was outstanding as the Bradford Senior Academy took the Championship in 2004.{{Clarify|date=August 2009}} In 2005 he continued his progress and made 21 appearances scoring 17 tries making him third in Bradford's try scoring charts for the year. As [[Super League]] champions Bradford faced [[National Rugby League]] premiers [[Wests Tigers]] in the [[2006 World Club Challenge]]. Pryce played at stand-off half back in the Bulls' 30–10 victory. Pryce was called up to the Great Britain squad in 2006 for the 2006 Tri nations although he did not feature in the competition. Pryce re-fractured a bone in his left foot on his Premiership début in March 2007 and had a pin inserted in his foot. He missed the rest of the season. For the 2007–08 season, Pryce, like other England Academy players at Gloucester, was dual-registered with [[Moseley Rugby Football Club|Moseley]], and could play for either club during the season. On 18 December 2007, Wigan announced that they had signed Pryce for the start of the new 2008 Super League Season, although he missed the majority of this season due to an ankle fracture injury, which had also hampered his time at Gloucester. On 10 December, Wigan announced Pryce's début would come in a friendly fixture against [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] on 28 December.{{cite news|title=Pryce poised to make Wigan début|date=10 December 2008|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/wigan/7775029.stm|work=BBC Sport}} Wigan won 44–4, with Pryce scoring two tries. Pryce made his return to [[Super League]] on 20 February 2009 against [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]]. Despite Castleford winning the game 22–28 Karl Pryce scored a single try. Pryce featured in the Challenge cup 4th round tie against [[Barrow Raiders|Barrow]] scoring a try. Pryce has played the majority of the season in Wigan's Reserve team.{{When|date=August 2009}} He made 14 appearances scoring 20 tries and has played in the centres, wing and stand off.{{When|date=August 2009}} ===2010s=== In 2012 [[Mick Potter]] signed Pryce on a 1-year deal for the 2012 season. The 25-year-old can play Wing, Centre and Second Row. Pryce did not feature in any of the pre-season games because of a [[hamstring]] injury. Karl missed Rounds 1–4 due to an injury. He featured in five consecutive games from Round 6 ([[Hull Kingston Rovers]]) to Round 10 ([[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]]). Pryce returned in Round 12 ([[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]]) to Round 23 ([[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]]). Pryce also played in Round 25 ([[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]]) and Round 26 ([[Hull F.C.]]). He was injured for Round 11, 27 and Round 4 of the [[Challenge Cup]]. He also featured against [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] in the [[Challenge Cup]]. Pryce scored against [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] (1 try), [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] (1 try), [[Hull F.C.]] (1 try), [[London Broncos]] (1 try), [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] (3 tries), [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] (2 tries), [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] (1 try), [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] (1 try) and [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] (2 tries). He was released at the end of the season. ===Statistics=== {|class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;" |- bgcolor=#bdb76b ! Season !! Appearance !! Tries !! Goals !! F/G !!
